description: Add shared canonical email-label constants, primary-email label metadata, and additional-email row interfaces while retaining scalar `contact.email` as the primary/default email.
Keep `@alga-psa/types` and other cross-package contact contracts in parity with the hybrid primary-plus-additional email model.
Add primary-email label metadata columns to `contacts` so the default email stored in `contacts.email` carries a label.
Create a tenant-scoped email-label definitions table reusable by both primary and additional contact emails.
Create an ordered `contact_additional_email_addresses` table that stores only non-default contact email addresses.
Enforce tenant-wide normalized-email uniqueness across both `contacts.email` and `contact_additional_email_addresses`.
Backfill existing contacts with safe primary-email label metadata without changing current `contacts.email` values or required-email behavior.
Validate create/update payloads for primary-email labels and additional-email rows, rejecting duplicates, malformed values, and additional rows that repeat the primary email.
Persist and hydrate primary-email label metadata and additional-email rows transactionally in the shared contact model.

Implement transactional default-swap behavior that promotes an additional email into `contacts.email`, demotes the old primary email into the child table, and disallows deleting the primary email directly.
Auto-create, reuse, and clean up tenant-scoped custom email labels for both primary and additional contact emails.

Update contact domain-event builders and full-contact workflow contracts to include primary-email label metadata and additional-email rows while keeping summary email fields sourced from `contacts.email`.
Create a shared `ContactEmailAddressesEditor` that renders the primary/default email row plus additional email rows with add/remove/reorder/promote interactions.
Support canonical email labels plus tenant-scoped custom-label suggestions in the shared email editor, mirroring the phone-label UX.
Wire contact create/edit/detail surfaces to the hybrid email model, including primary label editing and rendering of additional emails.
Wire quick-add contact creation flows, including inline contact creation in client flows, to author the hybrid primary-plus-additional email payload.
Keep list, picker, and summary surfaces anchored on `contacts.email` while exposing the richer email model where detailed contact rendering is needed.

Update contact query actions and `ContactService` list/search/filter/sort/export behavior to match both primary and additional emails while returning/displaying `contacts.email` as the summary address.
Update contact CSV template, mapping, preview, import, and export behavior for primary-email labels plus additional email rows.

Update shared `findContactByEmailAddress` and `createOrFindContactByEmail` helpers to search both primary and additional emails while creating new contacts with only a primary email in `contacts.email`.
Update shared `EmailService.findContactByEmail` and `EmailService.createOrFindContact` to understand the hybrid contact-email model and preserve both matched email and primary email where needed.
Update shared workflow email actions to search both primary and additional contact emails while creating new contacts with a primary email only.
Update `processInboundEmailInApp` so contact matching can hit an additional email while preserving the exact matched sender email separately from `contacts.email`.
Update runtime email-action schemas and workflow mappings so matched inbound email and primary/default contact email remain unambiguous under the hybrid model.
Update workflow business-operation contact lookup/search and related CRM summaries to search additional emails while keeping summary email fields backed by `contacts.email`.
Update REST contact schemas, controllers, and API services to accept and return primary-email label metadata plus additional email rows without removing scalar `email`.
Update n8n contact node fields, parsers, docs, and examples for scalar primary email plus additional email rows.
Update integration and client-lookup helpers that resolve contacts by email so they search both the primary email column and additional-email rows.

Update external sync, import, and export adapters only where contact parsing or lookup-by-email must understand additional contact emails, while preserving `contacts.email` as the default summary/send address.
Update factories, seeds, fixtures, and shared test helpers to create contacts with a labeled primary email and optional additional-email rows.
Preserve compatibility for existing `contacts.email` consumers, including notifications, portal/auth, billing, watcher, and scheduling flows, under the default-swap model.
